title: QUIC Hosted Interop-Runner Supported-Subset Coverage Architecture
purpose: Describe the advisory hosted matrix profile that collects external-runner artifacts for already-supported interop helper cells without changing protocol support or ordinary CI gates.
design_summary: The hosted workflow remains `workflow_dispatch` only. It adds a `coverage_profile` choice input whose default preserves the existing one-cell `hosted-handshake` behavior, while the explicit `supported-subset` profile runs the hosted handshake job and fans out an additional matrix over selected helper-supported cells: same-slot retry through the local replacement slot, split-role transfer in both local client and local server modes, and client-role multiconnect against quic-go with the helper's existing `multiconnect` to `handshakeloss` runner mapping. Each attempted cell invokes the existing repo-local helper, writes to a cell-specific artifact root, and uploads that cell's artifact bundle with `if: always()` so success, advisory, and failure outcomes all preserve evidence.
alternatives_considered: Run the supported-subset profile on every push or pull request. Rejected because interop-runner coverage is advisory, expensive, externally dependent, and not yet a support-readiness gate.

Expose `post-handshake-stream` through the external-runner helper as part of the matrix. Rejected because that testcase remains a local child-process harness proof and does not have an external-runner helper mapping in this slice.

Treat a matrix pass as broad interop readiness. Rejected because the profile covers selected helper cells only and still depends on external runner behavior, peer images, and preserved artifact inspection.
edge_cases_and_constraints: The default manual dispatch must remain the existing narrow hosted-handshake cell to avoid surprising expensive fan-out.
The wider matrix must be opt-in and advisory, with `fail-fast` disabled so one cell does not prevent evidence from other attempted cells.
Each cell must upload a distinct artifact bundle so partial evidence is attributable to the role, peer, and testcase combination that produced it.
The workflow must not add a runner registry entry or imply upstream runner support beyond image replacement.
data_and_state_considerations: Hosted matrix cells write transient artifacts under `artifacts/interop-runner/<cell>/` and upload them as GitHub Actions artifacts. The workflow does not commit generated runner reports, alter the external runner checkout, or persist transport state beyond uploaded artifacts.
risks: External runner, Docker, Python, or peer image drift can fail individual cells even when local helper planning and the library test baseline are green.
The supported-subset profile may be overread as public interop readiness unless the advisory boundary remains explicit.
Uploading several runner bundles can be larger and slower than the default handshake cell.

supplemental_sections.heading: Scope
supplemental_sections.content: This design covers only hosted workflow orchestration for already-supported helper cells. It does not change protocol runtime behavior, the public API surface, helper testcase support, or the external runner repository.
